room.name: a basement maintenance corridor.
room.description: A low concrete passage runs beneath the Ministry, lit by bare bulbs behind wire cages. At one end a barred steel hatch is bolted shut. Behind it you can see a manhole with an iron runged ladder leading downwards. Steam pipes rattle along the ceiling, their labels peeling with age. The air smells of ash and damp paper.

room.name: a service stairwell labelled basement access.
room.description: A narrow concrete stairwell rises toward the administrative levels above. The walls are bare except for a single telescreen mounted high out of reach, its lens is dark.

room.name: a furnace access chamber.
room.description: Heavy iron doors line the walls, each marked with soot-darkened warning stencils. Heat bleeds through the metal, and somewhere beyond you hear the steady roar of fire. Ash drifts across the floor like black snow.

room.name: the Minitrue street entrance checkpoint.
room.description: Glass doors lead out to the street, flanked by uniformed guards. Identification notices are plastered everywhere. Anyone entering or leaving pauses here, however briefly, under the unblinking eye of the telescreen. The security guard bars your way into the building.

room.name: the Ministry of Truth lobby.
room.description: A vast marble-floored hall stretches out beneath a ceiling lost in shadow. Party slogans are carved directly into the stone walls. Clerks move through metal detectors while guards watch from behind mirrored glass. A telescreen dominates the far wall, broadcasting the current bulletin.

room.name: a records intake corridor.
room.description: Metal carts stacked with documents line the walls, each tagged for revision or destruction. The corridor smells of ink and hot dust. A guard sits at a desk, barely glancing up as people pass.

room.name: an administrative corridor.
room.description: A narrow corridor lined with doors leading to clerical offices and filing rooms. The walls are covered with charts and notices of daily quotas. Telescreens hum quietly above each doorway, tracking movement.

room.name: outside Studio A.
room.description: A narrow passage flanked by the doors of recording studios. Studio A is to the south, a quiet space humming with equipment. The corridor continues north toward other work areas.

room.name: outside the 2 Minutes Hate auditorium.
room.description: The corridor stretches further west from the administrative areas. Faded posters of enemy leaders are plastered along the walls. Telescreens show looping propaganda clips.

room.name: the 2 Minutes Hate auditorium.
room.description: A vast hall with tiered seating and a stage at the far end. Large telescreens cover the front wall, each pulsing with the Party's latest denunciations. The crowd below shifts nervously, caught between fear and excitement.

room.name: outside the document lift (Ground floor).
room.description: The button to call the lift sits to the right of the doors. Currently it's dark but it's still visibly grimy with years of use. Stencilled glyphs next to the doors indicate furnaces basement, editorial level 1, prolesec and creative level 2, archives level 3, other areas are restricted inner party only. A faint draft rises from the shaft.

room.name: a service stairwell labelled ground floor access.
room.description: A narrow concrete stairwell rises toward the administrative levels above. The walls are bare except for a single telescreen mounted high out of reach, its lens dark but faintly humming.

room.name: the document lift.
room.description: The interior of the lift is grimy and scarred by years of heavy use, although the telescreens on either side are polished and well maintained. The doors close as soon as you step inside. It is brightly lit, there is no hiding in here.

room.name: outside the document lift (first floor)
room.description: The air here smells of paper, ink, dust and overheating circuitry. Directional arrows have been painted over repeatedly, their meanings subtly altered.

room.name: a service stairwell labelled first floor access
room.description: The concrete stairwell opens onto this level with no ceremony. Your footsteps echo loudly off the brutal concrete walls, it's unsettling and you become self conscious. A telescreen watches the landing, its surface dark.

room.name: the editorial main corridor
room.description: A long corridor stretches east and west, lined with identical doors. Each bears a department code rather than a name. The constant clatter of speakwrites leaks through the walls.

room.name: an open-plan revision office
room.description: Rows of narrow desks fill the room, each equipped with a speakwrite and telescreen. Clerks murmur corrections into their machines, barely pausing to breathe. Waste chutes along the walls swallow outdated truths without comment.

room.name: a sub-editors' review room
room.description: This room is quieter, though no less tense. Here, revised articles are checked for tone, emphasis, and ideological clarity. A red indicator light flashes whenever a telescreen flags an inconsistency.

room.name: your editorial cubicle
room.description: This cramped workspace is assigned to you. A speakwrite waits patiently on the desk, its indicator light glowing. The telescreen above you is positioned so it can see both your face and your hands. A schedule on the wall lists today's required revisions.

room.name: outside the document lift (second floor)
room.description: The lift doors open onto a quieter corridor than below. The air smells faintly of ink and cheap binding glue. Notices here are fewer, more abstract, their slogans harder to parse.

room.name: a service stairwell labelled second floor access
room.description: The stairwell door opens onto this level with a dull thud. The concrete here is cleaner, newer. A telescreen watches the landing, its surface dark but warm to the touch.

room.name: the creative publications corridor
room.description: A wide corridor lined with glass-fronted offices. Posters advertising poetry readings, approved novels and adult entertainment peel from the walls. The sound of comrades murmering into speakwrites from nearby offices mixes with low conversation.

room.name: the Prolesec literature office
room.description: Shelves of cheaply bound pamphlets line the walls. Folk songs, moral tales, simplified histories. A clerk stamps approval marks without reading.

room.name: an approved fiction workspace
room.description: Desks are scattered with manuscripts covered in red corrections. Stories here always end the same way. A telescreen hums softly, recording every keystroke.

room.name: a cultural revision office
room.description: This office handles songs, slogans, and children's rhymes. Old verses are crossed out, replaced with simpler ones that . A waste chute stands open, swallowing discarded lines.

room.name: outside the document lift (third floor)
room.description: The lift doors open onto a very quiet corridor. There's nothing here except for archives. Stencilled signs point to the records rooms to the West and meeting rooms to the East.

room.name: a service stairwell labelled third floor access
room.description: The stairwell opens onto straight out onto this level without ceremony. The concrete walls here look newer, recently painted a dull matt white. A telescreen watches the landing, its surface dark but warm to the touch.

room.name: the main corridor outside the processing office
room.description: The corridor stretches off to the west with closed doors leading off to the south and east.

room.name: the records processing office
room.description: There are several desks strewn with the accoutrements of office life, rubber stamps, in-trays. A memory hole is set back in the wall by each desk leading down to the furnaces in the basement. The telescreen is dark and silent but its eye is red, alive, silently watching the workers.

room.name: the photographic indexing room
room.description: This is where photographic evidence is originally indexed, manipulated and cross-referenced with other documents. Several gunmetal grey filing cabinets are lined up along the walls each stenciled with a cryptic looking code. The telescreens here are silent but very much alive at least during the daytime.

room.name: the Records Supervisor's office
room.description: A large office with a single desk. The telescreen is clearly on but the sound is somehow switched off.

room.name: Records Room 26
room.description:
Rows of steel filing cabinets stretch from floor to ceiling, each drawer stencilled with dates, locations and case numbers. A long inspection table runs down the centre of the room beneath a harsh fluorescent strip.
Several cabinets stand slightly ajar, as though someone left in a hurry. The air smells of dust, chemical toner and overheated paper.

room.name: outside the photographic index room
room.description: The corridor stretches off to the west with closed doors leading off to the south and east.

room.name: meeting room 1
room.description: There's a large rectangular table with chairs arranged around it. A few gunmetal grey filing cabinets and a barred window are the only relief to the witewashed walls.

room.name: outside records room 26
room.description: The corridor stretches off to the East.
